name: Freshdesk API
what_it_does: Freshdesk is a customer support and helpdesk platform. Its REST API enables agents to create and manage support tickets, add notes and replies, manage contacts and companies, update ticket properties, and automate support workflows with webhooks.
use_cases: Automatically creating tickets from monitored channels (email, logs, alerts)
Triaging and routing tickets based on content analysis
Adding automated notes or suggested resolutions to tickets
Escalating or updating ticket priority based on SLA rules
Syncing customer data between Freshdesk and CRM systems
not_for: High-volume event streaming (webhooks are push-only, not guaranteed delivery at scale)
Complex internal IT service management (consider Freshservice instead)
Real-time chat or live support workflows
best_when: An agent monitors a system or channel and needs to create or update support tickets based on detected events or customer communications.
avoid_when: Your support volume is very high (millions of tickets/mo) and you need guaranteed-delivery event processing — Freshdesk webhooks can miss events.

auth.notes: API key-based auth using HTTP Basic Auth with the key as username and 'X' as password. Simple and consistent. No OAuth2 for agent-to-agent flows. No granular scopes — key grants full API access for the associated agent's role.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for up to 10 agents; basic ticketing features only
pricing.notes: Free tier has API access but limited features (no automations, limited reporting). Growth tier unlocks automations and advanced workflows needed for most agent integrations.

performance.rate_limits: 1000 requests/minute on most plans; varies by endpoint and plan tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors return JSON with a description field and sometimes a nested errors object. HTTP status codes are used correctly. Error detail is moderate — enough to debug common issues but sometimes lacks specificity on validation failures.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Ticket creation is not idempotent. Agents should check for existing tickets (by source/subject) before creating to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: No idempotency — duplicate ticket creation is easy on retry; implement deduplication by source ID
Rate limits vary by plan; agents on free tier get very low limits (unlikely to be documented per-endpoint)
Webhook delivery is not guaranteed — Freshdesk may drop events under load; use polling as fallback
API key has full access for that agent's permission level — use a dedicated low-privilege agent account
Pagination uses page-based (not cursor), so concurrent modifications can cause items to be missed
Custom fields require knowing the field ID in advance; fetch field metadata before bulk operations
Attachment uploads require multipart form-data, not JSON — different from most other endpoints

score_rationale: Freshdesk API is functional for basic agent ticket management with reasonable documentation and webhooks. However, no idempotency, no OAuth, no granular scopes, page-based pagination, and non-guaranteed webhook delivery limit its reliability for agents. Sufficient for medium-complexity helpdesk automation but not a best-in-class developer experience.

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth with HTTP Basic auth encoding. Domain-specific API URLs. SOC2 Type II, GDPR. No scope granularity — API key has full account access. PCI DSS for payments module.
